settings.title: Hot Cross Buns Are Back for the Season
settings.excerpt: Spiced, studded with citrus peel, and only around until the week after Easter.
settings.body:
It would not be spring at Crumbly without hot cross buns, and the first trays are out now.

We make ours with a soft enriched dough, plenty of cinnamon and nutmeg, and orange peel we candy in the kitchen. The cross is piped flour paste, not icing, the way we like it.

How we make ours

The dough is enriched with butter, milk, and egg, which is what gives the buns their soft, pillowy pull rather than a dry crumb. We candy the orange peel ourselves over a couple of days, because the jarred kind tends to taste of nothing but sugar. The spices go in generously, and we let the shaped buns proof slowly so they bake up light. The cross is a simple flour-and-water paste piped on before baking, so it sets into the top rather than sitting on it like sweet glaze.

They are best the day they are baked, ideally split and toasted with a little butter melting into the cut side. A few seconds under the grill wakes the spice back up if you are eating one the next morning.

Ordering for the weekend

  • They come by the half dozen and the dozen.
  • The season ends the week after Easter, then they are gone for the year.
  • Order ahead if you want a box held back, as weekends sell through quickly.

Order ahead for the weekend if you want a box held back. Once the season closes we put the candied peel away until next spring, so this is a short window.
